text: Thank you, it's been wonderful. I've certainly been blessed and I hope that you have as well. I believe that this verse so adequately summarizes the message of Christmas. In first John four and verse number 14, the Bible says this, and we have seen and do testify that the father sent the son to be the savior of the world. Isn't that a wonderful verse of scripture? As I read that verse, I find that the reason that Christ was sent is clearly declared for us and it is to be the savior of the world. That word savior is such an important word for us to understand. And we find that the word means one that saves or preserves, but properly can only be applied to the Lord Jesus Christ. This is according to the Noah Webster's 1828 dictionary. It says properly applied only to Jesus Christ, the redeemer, who has opened the way to everlasting salvation by his obedience and death and who is therefore called the savior by way of distinction, the savior of the world. Begin to think about this statement, this declaration in first John four and verse number 14. And we find that there's obviously a need for a savior. And this need is a universal need. He was born, but he didn't stay a baby. He lived a perfect, sinless life. He was born to die.
